compatibilityLabel: React DOM 19.2.6 DOM attribute/property serialization and mutation oracle

generation.method: checked runtime inventory plus exact npm tarballs extracted into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per mode, scenario, and probe kind

probeModes.reason: Development mode records React DOM host prop output plus warnings for invalid, unknown, and canonicalized attributes.
Production mode records host prop serialization and mutation output without development-only warnings.

scenarios.description: Common host attributes plus React aliases className and htmlFor on a label.
Boolean attributes and booleanish string attributes that React maps to DOM attributes.
data-* and aria-* attributes with string, number, boolean, and empty values.
Lowercase custom attributes, camel-case unknown props, and invalid aria names on a built-in element.
Custom element host props, including existing DOM properties and dash-cased attributes.
A two-phase update that changes, removes, and retains host attributes.

fakeDomSubstrate.purpose: exercise React DOM client mutation calls deterministically in Node without adding a DOM dependency to the conformance workspace
fakeDomSubstrate.browserAttributeNameNormalizationMode: HTML namespace setAttribute/removeAttribute names are lowercased by the fake DOM before final tree snapshots
doesNotModel: layout, CSS cascade, focus, selection, and accessibility tree behavior
custom element lifecycle callbacks and browser upgrade timing
controlled form wrappers, selection restoration, and value tracking

args.value: A component is `contentEditable` and contains `children` managed by React. It is now your responsibility to guarantee that none of those nodes are unexpectedly modified or duplicated. This is probably not intentional.

args.value: Invalid aria prop %s on <%s> tag. For details, see https://react.dev/link/invalid-aria-props

React does not recognize the `%s` prop on a DOM element. If you intentionally want it to appear in the DOM as a custom attribute, spell it as lowercase `%s` instead. If you accidentally passed it from a parent component, remove it from the DOM element.

intentionalGaps.reason: This oracle records pinned React DOM behavior only; Fast React React DOM mutation behavior is not implemented in this worker.

Client probes execute real React DOM 19.2.6 mutation code, but the DOM target is a deterministic fake DOM. The oracle records React DOM calls and final fake DOM state, not browser layout, parser, CSSOM, focus, or custom-element reactions.
Controlled inputs/selects/textareas/forms, style diffing, dangerouslySetInnerHTML, event delegation, SVG, MathML, hydration, resources, and server Fizz markers have separate oracle tracks.
